body:
### Problem

Since 5181984db990111c76d7d7bceb3d7acba7da4406, I've been getting this error message when trying to delete a buffer in another tabpage that has an attached in-memory LSP client:

```
Error in "msg_ruler" UI event handler (ns=nvim.ui2):
Lua: vim/_core/ui2:105: E1159: Cannot open a float when closing the buffer
stack traceback:
	[C]: in function 'nvim_open_win'
	vim/_core/ui2:105: in function 'check_targets'
	vim/_core/ui2:160: in function 'ui_callback'
	vim/_core/ui2:210: in function <vim/_core/ui2:202>
	[C]: in function 'nvim__redraw'
	/usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua:1147: in function </usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua:1145>
	[C]: in function 'nvim_exec_autocmds'
	/usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua:765: in function 'reset'
	/usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/client.lua:1439: in function '_on_detach'
	/usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua:969: in function </usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua:966>
	[C]: in function 'nvim_buf_delete'
	[string "/home/strigoi/.config/nvim/test.lua"]:42: in function <[string "/home/strigoi/.config/nvim/test.lua"]:42>

```

I can't figure out what the *actual* issue is, but using `schedule_wrap` here removes the error:

```diff
diff --git a/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua b/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua
index a7f29029c9..dec2fb54cc 100644
--- a/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua
+++ b/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua
@@ -1141,11 +1141,11 @@

 api.nvim_create_autocmd('DiagnosticChanged', {
   group = api.nvim_create_augroup('nvim.diagnostic.status', {}),
-  callback = function(ev)
+  callback = vim.schedule_wrap(function(ev)
     if api.nvim_buf_is_loaded(ev.buf) then
       api.nvim__redraw({ buf = ev.buf, statusline = true })
     end
-  end,
+  end),
   desc = 'diagnostics component for the statusline',
 })
```

Disabling `ui2` or detaching the LSP client before `bd` also removes the error.

### Steps to reproduce

`test.lua`

```lua
-- Disabling ui2 -> no error
require('vim._core.ui2').enable {
  enable = true,
  msg = {
    target = 'cmd',
  },
}

-- From `:h lsp-server`
local function cmd_fn(dispatchers)
  local closing = false
  local request_id = 0

  local srv = {}
  function srv.request(method, params, callback)
    if method == 'initialize' then
      callback(nil, {
        capabilities = {
          hoverProvider = true,
        },
      })
    elseif method == 'shutdown' then
      callback(nil, nil)
    end
    request_id = request_id + 1
    return true, request_id
  end
  function srv.notify(method, params)
    if method == 'exit' then dispatchers.on_exit(0, 15) end
  end
  function srv.is_closing() return closing end
  function srv.terminate() closing = true end

  return srv
end

local buf = vim.api.nvim_create_buf(true, true)
local client_id = vim.lsp.start({ cmd = cmd_fn, name = 'my-server', root_dir = vim.uv.cwd() }, { bufnr = buf })

vim.api.nvim_open_tabpage(buf, true, {})

vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('WinClosed', {
  callback = function(ev)
    -- Detaching client before `bd` -> no error
    -- vim.lsp.buf_detach_client(buf, client_id)
    if ev.buf == buf then vim.api.nvim_buf_delete(ev.buf, {}) end
  end,
})
```

To reproduce:
* `nvim --clean -c 'e test.lua'`
* `:%lua`
* `:q` in the created buffer


### Expected behavior

Since closing a buffer automatically detaches LSP clients, I would expect this to work even without the potential fixes mentioned in `test.lua`. If I'm doing something wrong in my example feel free to close the issue, I reported this just in case it was unintended behavior.

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

commit 5181984

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

n/a

### Operating system/version

Linux 7.0.9-arch1-1

### Terminal name/version

wezterm

### $TERM environment variable

xterm-256color

### Installation

build at specified commit or later

comment_data.body:
~~`aucmd_prepbuf` uses a floatwin, so I guess that's the floating window the error message is referring to.~~

> I can't figure out what the _actual_ issue is, but using `schedule_wrap` here removes the error:

does it work if you schedule this instead (which at least makes more semantic sense): https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/7662001c482d63e1119aafcebc0e13d1201a2805/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua#L765

> so I guess that's the floating window the error message is referring to

Do you not read the stacktrace?

This works:

```diff
diff --git a/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua b/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua
index a7f29029c9..b2782c2a87 100644
--- a/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua
+++ b/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua
@@ -760,15 +760,17 @@
       M.hide(iter_namespace, iter_bufnr)
     end

-    if api.nvim_buf_is_valid(iter_bufnr) then
-      api.nvim_exec_autocmds('DiagnosticChanged', {
-        modeline = false,
-        buf = iter_bufnr,
-        data = { diagnostics = {} },
-      })
-    else
-      M._store.drop_buf(iter_bufnr)
-    end
+    vim.schedule(function()
+      if api.nvim_buf_is_valid(iter_bufnr) then
+        api.nvim_exec_autocmds('DiagnosticChanged', {
+          modeline = false,
+          buf = iter_bufnr,
+          data = { diagnostics = {} },
+        })
+      else
+        M._store.drop_buf(iter_bufnr)
+      end
+    end)
   end
 end
```

This does not:

```diff
diff --git a/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua b/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua
index a7f29029c9..3b73846425 100644
--- a/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua
+++ b/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua
@@ -761,11 +761,13 @@
     end

     if api.nvim_buf_is_valid(iter_bufnr) then
-      api.nvim_exec_autocmds('DiagnosticChanged', {
-        modeline = false,
-        buf = iter_bufnr,
-        data = { diagnostics = {} },
-      })
+      vim.schedule(function()
+        api.nvim_exec_autocmds('DiagnosticChanged', {
+          modeline = false,
+          buf = iter_bufnr,
+          data = { diagnostics = {} },
+        })
+      end)
     else
       M._store.drop_buf(iter_bufnr)
     end
```

> or maybe scheduled_ui_callback should have been called instead of ui_callback ?

Yes scheduling the `msg_ruler` callback is probably fine in general and would avoid the error in this repro. A more comprehensive fix might be to check for errors creating windows/buffers (at the risk of showing events too late in some cases):
```diff
diff --git a/runtime/lua/vim/_core/ui2.lua b/runtime/lua/vim/_core/ui2.lua
index 619abde1c4..d029d61878 100644
--- a/runtime/lua/vim/_core/ui2.lua
+++ b/runtime/lua/vim/_core/ui2.lua
@@ -157,7 +157,6 @@ end
 
 local function ui_callback(redraw_msg, event, ...)
   local handler = M.msg[event] or M.cmd[event] --[[@as function]]
-  M.check_targets()
   handler(...)
   -- Cmdline mode, non-fast message and non-empty showcmd require an immediate redraw.
   if M.cmd[event] or redraw_msg or (event == 'msg_showcmd' and select(1, ...)[1]) then
@@ -203,8 +202,9 @@ function M.enable(opts)
     if not (M.msg[event] or M.cmd[event]) then
       return
     end
+    local ok = pcall(M.check_targets)
     -- Ensure cmdline is placed after a scheduled message in block mode.
-    if vim.in_fast_event() or (event == 'cmdline_show' and M.cmd.srow > 0) then
+    if not ok or vim.in_fast_event() or (event == 'cmdline_show' and M.cmd.srow > 0) then
       scheduled_ui_callback(false, event, ...)
     else
       ui_callback(event == 'msg_show', event, ...)
```

Other than that the ruler event seems to be a remnant ruler that is unwanted. I.e. also without UI2 there is a leftover ruler in the cmdline that should have been cleared (or never drawn in the first place):
```diff
diff --git a/src/nvim/statusline.c b/src/nvim/statusline.c
index f458efd8a7..c831e64b0d 100644
--- a/src/nvim/statusline.c
+++ b/src/nvim/statusline.c
@@ -9,6 +9,7 @@
 #include "nvim/api/private/defs.h"
 #include "nvim/api/private/helpers.h"
 #include "nvim/ascii_defs.h"
+#include "nvim/autocmd.h"
 #include "nvim/buffer.h"
 #include "nvim/buffer_defs.h"
 #include "nvim/charset.h"
@@ -440,7 +441,7 @@ void win_redr_winbar(win_T *wp)
 void redraw_ruler(void)
 {
   static int did_ruler_col = -1;
-  win_T *wp = curwin->w_status_height == 0 ? curwin : lastwin_nofloating(NULL);
+  win_T *wp = !is_aucmd_win(curwin) && curwin->w_status_height == 0 ? curwin : lastwin_nofloating(NULL);
   bool is_stl_global = global_stl_height() > 0;

   // Check if ruler should be drawn, clear if it was drawn before.
```

labels.description: wrong behavior that was introduced in a previous commit (please bisect)
labels.name: bug-regression

labels.description: Nvim 0.12 mesages/dialog/pager/notifications based on the UI-ext protocol
labels.name: ui2

title: ui2 + diagnostic: `E1159: Cannot open a float when closing the buffer`
